description: Implement metric filtering framework — namepass/namedrop/tagpass/tagdrop/fieldpass/fielddrop with glob matching
steps: Create src/core/metric-filter.ts
Implement glob-to-regex compilation (support * and ? wildcards)
Implement MetricFilter class with apply(metric) method
Implement namepass: pass only metrics matching any pattern
Implement namedrop: drop metrics matching any pattern
Implement tagpass: pass only metrics with matching tag key+values
Implement tagdrop: drop metrics with matching tag key+values
Implement fieldpass: keep only matching fields (remove others)
Implement fielddrop: remove matching fields (keep others)
Implement evaluation order: namepass → namedrop → tagpass → tagdrop → fieldpass → fielddrop
Handle edge case: all fields removed after filtering → drop metric
Implement MetricFilterSchema (Zod) for config validation
Write comprehensive tests
verification: All 6 filter types work correctly with glob patterns. Edge cases handled. All tests pass.
tests: No filters configured → metric passes through unchanged
namepass: matching name → passes; non-matching → dropped
namedrop: matching name → dropped; non-matching → passes
namepass + namedrop together: whitelist first, then blacklist
tagpass: matching tag value → passes; non-matching value → dropped
tagpass: metric missing the tag key → dropped
tagdrop: matching tag value → dropped
fieldpass: only matching fields kept, others removed
fielddrop: matching fields removed, others kept
fieldpass removes all fields → metric dropped entirely
Glob wildcard *: temperature_* matches temperature_motor
Glob wildcard ?: temp_? matches temp_1 but not temp_12
Multiple patterns: [temp_*, pressure_*] matches either
Empty filter arrays → no filtering (pass everything)
Case-sensitive matching: Temperature ≠ temperature

description: Integration test: metric filtering applied in pipeline context
steps: Create test/integration/metric-filter-pipeline.test.ts
Test filtering applied to input metrics in pipeline
Test filtering applied to processor output
Verify filtered metrics don't reach output
verification: Metric filtering works within the full pipeline. All tests pass.
tests: Input with namepass filter: only matching metrics reach output
Processor with fieldpass: only specified fields survive to output
Combined: input namepass + processor fieldpass both applied

description: Implement rename processor — rename fields and tags on metrics
steps: Create src/plugins/processors/rename.ts
Implement Zod config schema with replace rules array
Implement process(): apply rename rules, emit via acc.addMetric()
Implement field rename: remove old field, add new field with same value
Implement tag rename: remove old tag, add new tag with same value
Handle missing source field/tag: skip rule silently
Handle multiple rename rules applied in order
Ensure hashId updates after tag rename (tag sort order changes)
Write tests
verification: Rename processor correctly renames fields and tags. All tests pass.
tests: Rename field: temperature → motor_temp_c
Rename tag: host → hostname
Source field not present → rule skipped, metric still forwarded
Multiple rename rules applied in order
Rename doesn't affect other fields/tags
Metric forwarded via acc.addMetric() (explicit emit)
Tag rename: hashId changes (tags re-sorted)
Config validation: dest is required, empty replace array is valid

description: Integration test: rename processor in full pipeline
steps: Create test/integration/rename-pipeline.test.ts
Configure pipeline with mock input → rename processor → mock output
Verify renamed fields appear in output
Verify original field names absent from output
verification: Rename processor transforms metrics correctly in pipeline. All tests pass.
tests: Input metric with field 'temperature' → output has 'motor_temp_c' (no 'temperature')
Multiple rename rules: both applied in output metrics
Metric without matching field: passes through unchanged

description: Implement filter processor — drop/pass metrics by name, tag, and field criteria
steps: Create src/plugins/processors/filter.ts
Implement Zod config schema with all filter fields
Implement process(): apply MetricFilter, emit if passes, drop if not
Write tests
verification: Filter processor correctly passes/drops metrics. All tests pass.
tests: namepass: matching metrics pass, non-matching dropped
namedrop: matching metrics dropped
tagpass: metrics with matching tags pass
Combined namepass + tagdrop: both applied
No filters configured → all metrics pass (no-op)
fieldpass: only specified fields kept
All fields filtered → metric dropped entirely

description: Integration test: filter processor in pipeline
steps: Create test/integration/filter-pipeline.test.ts
Configure pipeline with mock input → filter processor → mock output
Verify only matching metrics reach output
Test with mixed metrics: some pass, some dropped
verification: Filter processor correctly gates metrics in pipeline. All tests pass.
tests: Mixed input: 5 metrics, namepass allows 3 → output receives 3
tagdrop removes metrics with test=true tag
fieldpass trims fields: output metrics have only specified fields

description: Implement basicstats aggregator — min/max/mean/count/sum/variance/stdev over time windows
steps: Create src/plugins/aggregators/basicstats.ts
Implement Zod config schema (period, drop_original, stats, namepass/namedrop)
Implement add(metric): accumulate numeric field values, group by hashId
Implement running statistics: use Welford's algorithm for numerically stable variance
Implement push(acc): emit summary metrics via acc.addFields()
Implement reset(): clear accumulated state
Handle multiple series: separate stats per name+tags group
Handle mixed field types: aggregate number/bigint, skip string/boolean
Handle BigInt fields: convert to Number for aggregation (warn if > MAX_SAFE_INTEGER)
Handle empty window: push() emits nothing
Implement configurable stats selection (only emit requested stats)
Preserve original tags on summary metrics
Write comprehensive tests
verification: Basicstats computes correct statistics. Multiple series tracked independently. All tests pass.
tests: 10 numeric values → correct min, max, mean, count, sum
Multiple series (different tags) → separate stats per series
Mixed field types: number aggregated, string/boolean ignored
BigInt field aggregated (converted to Number)
Empty window → push() emits nothing
reset() clears state — next push() reflects only new data
Stats config: stats=[count, mean] → only count and mean emitted
Summary metric preserves original tags
Summary field names: {field}_min, {field}_max, {field}_mean, etc.
Single value: min=max=mean=value, count=1, variance=0, stdev=0
Variance and stdev correct (Welford's algorithm)
namepass on aggregator: only matching metrics accumulated
Config validation: period is valid duration string

description: Integration test: basicstats aggregator in full pipeline — E2E with real timer
steps: Create test/integration/basicstats-pipeline.test.ts
Configure pipeline with mock input → basicstats aggregator → mock output
Let pipeline run for > 1 aggregation period
Verify both original metrics AND summary metrics in output
Verify summary stats are correct
Test with drop_original=true: only summaries in output
verification: Basicstats works in full pipeline with real timer. Summaries correct. All tests pass.
tests: Originals + summaries both arrive at output (drop_original=false)
Summary min/max/mean/count match expected values from input
drop_original=true: only summary metrics in output (no originals)
Multiple aggregation periods: stats reset between windows
